summary: Added a C decompilation for two functions from MSL/buffer_io.s, introduced a supporting buffer_io.h with provisional struct and bitfield layouts, and switched obj_files.mk from the assembly object to src/MSL/buffer_io.c.o. The PR had no recorded review comments, so the evidence is limited to the title, body, changed files, and diff.
what_changed: Created src/MSL/buffer_io.c with implementations of __flush_buffer and __prep_buffer.
Created src/MSL/buffer_io.h containing BufferIoUnkFlagStruct and BufferIoUnkStruct definitions used by the new C functions.
Changed obj_files.mk so TEXT_O_FILES uses $(BUILD_DIR)/src/MSL/buffer_io.c.o instead of $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/MSL/buffer_io.s.o.

__flush_buffer computes the pending byte count from x20 - x18, conditionally calls the x3C callback with x00, x18, &x24, and x44, optionally writes the transferred count through arg1, updates x14 on success, then resets x20, x24, and x30.
__prep_buffer resets the active buffer pointer/count fields x20, x24, and x30 based on x18, x1C, x14, and x28.
smart_moves: Kept the unknown MSL data layout explicit with offset-based field names, filler fields, and comments, preserving ABI-sensitive structure offsets while still allowing C to match the assembly.
Modeled byte flags as a nested one-byte bitfield struct so code could access arg0->x05.bits.b4 at the observed offset without over-naming behavior that was not yet understood.
Used a function pointer field at offset 0x3C with a concrete signature matching the observed call pattern in __flush_buffer.
Updated obj_files.mk in the same PR as the source addition, making the decompiled object replace the original assembly object in the build.
decomp_lessons: For MSL internals with incomplete semantic understanding, provisional names like BufferIoUnkStruct and xNN fields are acceptable when the layout and offsets are the important matching constraint.
When replacing an asm MSL file, include the obj_files.mk swap so the build actually compiles the new C object instead of the old .s object.
Offset comments on struct fields are valuable in low-level library decompilation because small layout mistakes can change generated code and break matching.
Callback fields should be typed from observed callsites first; stronger semantic names can wait until more of the surrounding subsystem is understood.
The unusual statement `arg0 != 0;` appears to be a matching artifact inside the `arg0->x05.bits.b4` branch, demonstrating that no-op expressions may be retained when required by codegen evidence.
naming_conventions: MSL internal functions keep double-underscore names matching the original symbols: __flush_buffer and __prep_buffer.
Unknown structs use `Unk` in the type name, as in BufferIoUnkStruct and BufferIoUnkFlagStruct.
Unknown struct members are named by byte offset, such as x00, x14, x18, x20, x24, x28, x30, x3C, and x44.
Padding or not-yet-identified members use `_filler` suffixes, such as x08_filler, x2C_filler, x34_filler, and x40_filler.
Function pointer argument placeholders include the owning field name, e.g. x3C_arg0, x3C_arg1, x3C_arg2, and x3C_arg3.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced `asm/MSL/buffer_io.s.o` with `src/MSL/buffer_io.c.o` in TEXT_O_FILES after adding matching C.
Used explicit temporary locals `temp_r0` and `temp_r3`, reflecting register-oriented matching work.
Used casts to s32 around pointer/integer-like fields and arithmetic to control generated code shape.
Preserved a no-op comparison expression `arg0 != 0;` under a bitfield test, likely to influence or preserve matching codegen.
Represented status flags through u8 bitfields to access a specific bit at byte offset 0x05.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename BufferIoUnkStruct fields or identify the real MSL buffer structure type.
Search for later changes to __flush_buffer or __prep_buffer to see whether the `arg0 != 0;` matching artifact was removed or explained.
Check subsequent MSL direct_io or file_io decompilations for reuse of the same buffer struct layout.
Search for uses of x3C callback signature to infer the semantics of the buffer I/O write/read callback.
